title: 2fa
author: masterwolf-git
body:
Hello

I use 2fa with sys-auth/google-authenticator on gentoo, and it seems your application cannot interact with that.

title: Feature request , toggle extra information per host in the dashboard.
author: sosher
body:
First, thank you for this tool. It is very handy to have.

The metrics dashboard feature is awesome and genuinely helpful. That said, some hosts will not be able to provide this information because they are not running Bash, Zsh, or a similar shell environment. Examples include FortiGate firewalls and other network devices.

The constant login/logout behavior can also be an issue for some devices. It would be useful to have an option for ICMP checks or basic port reachability instead of repeatedly logging in. That would help avoid constantly bothering our cyber teams — even though, to be fair, they do drive us crazy sometimes, so it is nice when it is our turn.

Thanks again.

title: Hardening: Path traversal, resource leaks, performance
author: PowerAppsDarren
body:
Lower-severity findings from a code review — hardening opportunities and minor improvements.

## 1. Incomplete SFTP path traversal guard (ssh/sftp.rs ~L342)

`do_download` blocks literal `..` components but doesn't protect against symlinks pointing outside the intended directory or absolute paths. `do_upload` has no path validation at all.

**Suggestion:** Canonicalize the local path and verify it's under the expected base directory.

## 2. Temp file leak on rename failure (config/mod.rs ~L82)

`hosts.toml.tmp` is left on disk if `std::fs::rename` fails (e.g., cross-device rename). No data loss, but stale `.tmp` files accumulate.

**Suggestion:** Clean up the temp file in the error path:
```rust
if let Err(e) = std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path) {
    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&tmp_path);
    return Err(e).with_context(...);
}
```

## 3. `SftpDisconnected` sent with empty host_name (ssh/sftp.rs ~L174)

On `ListDir` failure, `SftpDisconnected` is sent with `host_name: String::new()`. The status bar shows the error with no host context.

**Suggestion:** Pass `host_name` into `sftp_task_loop` and use it in error events.

## 4. known_hosts read failure invisible to user (ssh/session.rs ~L83)

When `known_hosts` is unreadable, the connection is refused (correct fail-closed), but the only feedback is a `warn!` log. Users without logging enabled get cryptic connection failures.

**Suggestion:** Surface this as a status bar error event.

## 5. SSH config Include symlink cycle fallback (config/ssh_config.rs ~L110)

When `canonicalize()` fails on an Include path, the raw path is used for cycle detection, potentially allowing 3^3 = 27 redundant file reads with crafted symlinks.

**Suggestion:** Treat `canonicalize` failure as an unreadable file (skip it) rather than falling back to the raw path.

## 6. Sequential service metric collection (ssh/services/mod.rs ~L121)

`collect_all_metrics` iterates detected services sequentially. With 5 services, each issuing multiple SSH commands, latency compounds.

**Suggestion:** Use `futures::future::join_all` to collect metrics from all providers concurrently.

## 7. Event thread panic causes silent hang (event.rs ~L266)

The `JoinHandle` from `spawn_event_thread` is dropped. If the thread panics, the main loop blocks forever on `event_rx.recv()`.

**Suggestion:** Store the handle and check it periodically, or use `catch_unwind` inside the thread.

## 8. Dead code: `pending_connect` (app/mod.rs ~L357)

`pending_connect` is never set to `Some(...)` anywhere — `connect_system_ssh` is unreachable.

**Suggestion:** Remove the dead code path, or wire it up if the system-ssh fallback is still intended.

---

None of these are urgent, but they'd improve robustness. Happy to help with any!

title: Crypto & auth: TOFU notification, plaintext passwords, DSA keys
author: PowerAppsDarren
body:
Some authentication and cryptographic concerns from a code review.

## 1. Plaintext passwords in world-readable config file (config/mod.rs ~L82)

`hosts.toml` stores SSH passwords in cleartext. The file is written via `std::fs::write` + `std::fs::rename` without setting explicit permissions — it inherits the process umask (typically `022`), making it readable by all users on the system.

**Suggestion:** Create the file with `0600` permissions using `OpenOptions::new().mode(0o600)`. Apply the same fix to `snippets.toml` and `config.toml`. Longer term, consider OS keychain integration (`keyring` crate).

## 2. Password exposed via environment variable (ssh/pty.rs ~L132, ssh/askpass.rs)

`OMNY_ASKPASS_PASSWORD` is set as an env var on the spawned SSH process. On Linux, any same-UID process can read it via `/proc/<pid>/environ`. The variable also propagates transitively to all children of the SSH subprocess.

**Suggestion:** Use a Unix domain socket or pipe to pass the password to the askpass helper instead of an env var. Alternatively, zero out the env var in the askpass helper immediately after reading.

## 3. TOFU with no user notification (ssh/session.rs ~L48)

`check_server_key` silently accepts and records unknown host keys with no UI feedback. Standard `ssh` at least prints "The authenticity of host X can't be established..." before recording.

**Suggestion:** Show a status bar notification or popup with the host fingerprint on first-time key acceptance.

## 4. DSA key in default search list (ssh/session.rs ~L300)

`default_key_paths` includes `id_dsa`. DSA keys are limited to 1024 bits with SHA-1 and are cryptographically broken. OpenSSH disabled DSA by default in 7.0 (2015) and removed it in 9.8 (2024).

**Suggestion:** Remove `id_dsa` from the default list. If backward compatibility is needed, log a warning when a DSA key is found.

## 5. Generated keys have no passphrase (ssh/key_setup.rs ~L290)

Keys generated by OmnySSH use `-N ""` (empty passphrase). If the private key is stolen, the attacker gets persistent access to all servers where password auth was disabled.

**Suggestion:** Prompt the user to optionally set a passphrase during key generation, or document this limitation prominently in the UI.

## 6. Unsafe `impl Send` for `SendMasterPty` (ssh/pty.rs ~L39)

The safety argument holds for current code but has no compile-time enforcement. Any refactor that accidentally accesses the master from the reader thread introduces UB silently.

**Suggestion:** Wrap the resize path in `Mutex<Box<dyn MasterPty>>` to eliminate the `unsafe` entirely.

---

These are all opportunities to tighten the security posture. Happy to contribute PRs!

title: Bugs: Exit status lost, file manager issues, terminal restore
author: PowerAppsDarren
body:
Found several functional bugs during a code review. Grouped here for convenience.

## 1. Exit status lost when Eof arrives before ExitStatus (ssh/session.rs ~L395)

`collect_output` breaks on `Eof` before reading the `ExitStatus` channel message. Per RFC 4254 §6.10, some sshd implementations send `Eof` before `ExitStatus`. When this happens, the exit code is never read. `run_command_checked` treats `None` exit status as success, so key-setup verification commands could silently misreport failure.

**Suggestion:** Continue reading after `Eof` until `Close` or `None` arrives, only breaking on those.

## 2. Multi-file remote delete closes progress popup after first file (app/file_manager.rs ~L471)

`fm_delete` sends N `SftpCommand::Delete` commands without setting `pending_ops`. The `SftpOpDone` handler calls `saturating_sub(1)` on `pending_ops` (which is 0), so `remaining == 0` is immediately true and the popup closes after the first file finishes. The remaining deletes still execute but trigger N-1 redundant `ListDir` refreshes.

**Suggestion:** Set `pending_ops = paths.len()` before sending remote delete commands, matching the pattern used in `fm_paste`.

## 3. Local multi-file delete swallows all errors except the last (app/file_manager.rs ~L480)

```rust
if let Err(e) = result {
    last_err = Some(e.to_string());
}
```

If files 1 and 3 fail but file 2 succeeds, only file 3's error is reported. If file 3 succeeds after file 1 fails, the entire operation looks successful.

**Suggestion:** Collect all errors into a `Vec<String>` and report them all.

## 4. `SftpConnected` fires before `sftp_manager` is set (ssh/sftp.rs ~L131 + app/mod.rs ~L796)

`SftpManager::connect()` spawns a task that sends `AppEvent::SftpConnected`, but `self.sftp_manager` is still `None` at that point (set later by `SftpManagerReady`). The `ListDir` call in the `SftpConnected` handler always hits the `None` branch and is silently dropped.

**Suggestion:** Remove `SftpConnected` or merge it into `SftpManagerReady`.

## 5. Terminal restore fails if `disable_raw_mode()` errors (app/mod.rs ~L334)

The `?` propagates immediately, leaving the terminal in alternate screen with no cursor — user's shell is left broken.

**Suggestion:** Use `let _ = disable_raw_mode()` and continue to the restore calls unconditionally, then return the first error after all steps complete.

## 6. `docker inspect $(docker ps -aq)` hits ARG_MAX silently (ssh/services/docker.rs ~L71)

With hundreds of containers, the command-line expansion exceeds `ARG_MAX` (~2 MiB), `2>/dev/null` silences the error, and all restart-loop alerts are missed.

**Suggestion:** Use `docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.RestartCount}}'` instead.

## 7. Blocking `std::fs::remove_dir` on async worker (app/file_manager.rs ~L486)

Called inside `tokio::spawn` — can block the tokio worker thread on slow I/O.

**Suggestion:** Replace with `tokio::fs::remove_dir(&path).await`.

---

Great project overall — the architecture is clean. These are mostly edge cases. Happy to help with fixes!

title: Supply chain & CI: Unsigned binaries and unpinned actions
author: PowerAppsDarren
body:
A few supply chain and CI/CD findings from a code review.

## 1. Install script skips checksum verification (install.sh ~L107)

The install script downloads a binary from GitHub Releases and installs it via `sudo mv` without verifying the SHA256SUMS file that's already published in each release. A MITM or compromised CDN could serve a malicious binary.

**Suggestion:** After download, fetch and verify:
```sh
SHA_URL="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$VERSION/SHA256SUMS"
curl -fsSL "$SHA_URL" -o "$TMP_DIR/SHA256SUMS"
(cd "$TMP_DIR" && grep "$ARCHIVE_NAME" SHA256SUMS | sha256sum -c -)
```

## 2. Self-update uses unsigned checksums (update.rs ~L168)

`perform_update` downloads a release archive and SHA256SUMS from the same GitHub release. The SHA256 verification is implemented correctly, but the SUMS file itself has no independent signature (GPG/Sigstore). An attacker who can replace the release assets can replace both the binary and the SUMS file — the checksum passes against the tampered binary.

**Suggestion:** Sign releases with a GPG key or Sigstore and verify the signature in-app. Embed the signing public key at compile time.

## 3. GitHub Actions pinned to mutable tags (release.yml, ci.yml)

All `uses:` references use floating tags (`@v4`, `@v2`, `@stable`) rather than immutable commit SHAs:
```yaml
uses: actions/checkout@v4
uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
```

This is the same vector as the `tj-actions/changed-files` supply chain attack (March 2025). The `softprops/action-gh-release` action runs with `contents: write` in the publish job — a compromised tag could replace all release binaries.

**Suggestion:** Pin to full commit SHAs and use Dependabot for updates:
```yaml
uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
uses: softprops/action-gh-release@c95fe1489396fe8a9eb87f0958e2ca1b25ebc786  # v2.2.1
```

## 4. Man page fetched from mutable branch (install.sh)

The man page is fetched from `main` branch (`raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/main/doc/omny.1`) rather than the tagged release commit. It could silently diverge from the installed binary version.

**Suggestion:** Use `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/$REPO/$VERSION/doc/omny.1`.

---

Happy to help with PRs for any of these!

title: Security: Shell injection risks in remote command construction
author: PowerAppsDarren
body:
Hi! I've been reviewing the codebase and found a few security concerns in the shell-command-construction layer. Sharing in case they're useful.

## 1. Shell injection via public key content (key_setup.rs ~L383)

`build_authorized_keys_command` embeds the public key into a single-quoted `echo` command with only single-quote escaping. The comment field is built from `host.name` (user-supplied via the TUI) without sanitization before passing to `ssh-keygen -C`. If the resulting `.pub` file contains a newline or shell metacharacters, the echo command splits and the second line executes as arbitrary shell code on the remote host.

**Suggestion:** Validate the `.pub` file matches `^(ssh-ed25519|ssh-rsa|ecdsa-sha2-[^ ]+) [A-Za-z0-9+/=]+ ?[^\n]*$` after reading, or use SFTP to upload the key instead of shell echo.

## 2. sed regex injection in `force_sshd_directive` (key_setup.rs ~L446)

This function interpolates `directive` and `value` directly into a `sed` expression run under `sudo`. Currently only called with compile-time constants, but the function is `pub` — any future caller with user-supplied input gets RCE on the remote host. Characters like `/`, `\`, `&`, `[`, `]` in either argument would break or alter the sed expression.

**Suggestion:** Validate inputs match `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$`, or use an awk/python rewrite that avoids sed metacharacter issues.

## 3. Snippet parameter injection (app/snippets.rs ~L614)

`substitute_params` inserts user-supplied parameter values verbatim into shell commands with no escaping. The test `subst_value_inserted_verbatim_no_shell_escaping` explicitly pins this: `"; rm -rf /"` passes through unmodified. The substituted command is then executed on the remote host via `run_command`.

**Suggestion:** Shell-escape parameter values with something like `shlex::quote`, or document clearly that snippet parameters are shell-unsafe.

## 4. Broad `Include` directive disabling (key_setup.rs ~L429)

The hardening step comments out ALL `Include` directives in `sshd_config`, not just the cloud-init ones. An admin's legitimate `Include` for security policies (e.g., `AllowUsers` restrictions) would be silently disabled.

**Suggestion:** Only comment out `Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/` specifically, or warn the user before proceeding.

---

Found during a community code review. Happy to discuss or help with PRs if any of these are useful. Great project!

title: Unable to use Tab auto-completion for commands in the terminal window.

In the terminal environment, the Tab key is mapped as the shortcut for "Next Tab." 
Pressing Tab switches between terminal tabs instead of triggering command auto-completion.

title: brew install timhartmann7/omnyssh fails
author: iskerwin
body:
Hi,

I’m trying to install omnyssh via Homebrew, but the installation fails.

Command used:
`brew install timhartmann7/omnyssh`

Error message:
`Warning: No available formula or cask with the name "timhartmann7/tap/omnyssh". Did you mean timhartmann7/tap/omny?`

Environment:

* macOS version: macOS 26.4.1
* Homebrew version: 5.1.7-33-g9eeb71a
* Terminal: Ghostty
* Shell: zsh

title: Bug: Error output bleeds through TUI rendering
author: mikkelrask
body:
**Describe the bug**
When a host fails to respond in time (e.g. command timeout during metrics collection), the error output is written directly to the terminal instead of being captured and handled within the TUI. This causes raw text to render on top of the TUI layout, corrupting the display until the user resizes the terminal or presses `r` to force a redraw.

**To reproduce**
1. Add a host that is reachable via SSH but where non-interactive command execution is slow or times out (e.g. due to a misconfigured `ForceCommand`, slow DNS, or a high-latency connection)
2. Open the dashboard
3. Observe raw error/warning text bleeding through the TUI panels after 30s

**Expected behavior**
All output from background SSH operations (including errors and timeouts) should be captured internally and either displayed within the TUI (e.g. as a status message on the host card) or suppressed — never written directly to stdout/stderr in a way that corrupts the terminal display.

**Actual behavior**
Raw warning text such as `WARN omny::ssh::pool: quick scan failed host=... error=command timed out (30s)` is written directly to the terminal, overlapping with the TUI layout.

**Workaround**
Resizing the terminal for exampel redraws the TUI and clears the corrupted output.

**Environment**
- OS: Linux
- Terminal: ghostty
- OmnySSH version: 1.0.0
